title: Purely Pleasant Japanese Pea Eggplant Nabemono
description: A traditional Japanese Kyushu dish featuring pea eggplant, tempered chocolate, and a glassy jelly texture, suitable for lacto-vegetarian diets. This nabemono recipe is a unique and delicious meal prep option.
instructions: In a large pot, combine kombu dashi broth, mirin, soy sauce, and sake, and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce the heat to medium low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add sliced pea eggplant to the pot and cook for 5 minutes, or until tender.
In a small bowl, whisk together grated daikon and shichimi togarashi.
Add the daikon mixture to the pot and stir to combine.
In a separate saucepan, melt tempered dark chocolate over low heat, stirring constantly.
Add agar agar powder to the chocolate and stir to combine.
Add the chocolate agar mixture to the pot and stir to combine.
Continue to simmer the nabemono for an additional 5 minutes, or until the flavors have melded together and the jelly texture has formed.
Stir in sesame oil and serve hot.
